text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Qualcomm surging is lifting chip sentiment broadly, but SK Hynix's massive Nasdaq listing is raising competition concerns for the memory/semi space. Your concentrated chip book is caught in that crossfire.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest problem — down 12.77% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast. AVGO is your largest position, also down 12.45% on a TRIM signal — size plus loss plus margin is a dangerous combo. SPAL is down 14.48% — it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily; losses compound faster than you think, and your stop at $19 is close.

Watch: (1) How chip names react to the SK Hynix listing news — if it pressures AVGO or SMH, your book takes a hit. (2) SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — treat it as fragile until it proves itself.

Bottom line: PLTR and AVGO need a decision today — on margin, hoping for a bounce is a plan for a bigger loss.
